content: We can draw an unexpected parallel between how high school language learners build reflective writing skills through iterative vocabulary and argument refinement, and how large language models can improve their self-reflection mechanisms by adopting the graded reading and writing workshop structure of a high school English class. Specifically, LLM self-reflection can be split into sequential, scaffolded stages mirroring the vocabulary learning -> drafting -> peer feedback -> revision workflow of a Unit 9 writing workshop, where each stage targets a different layer of self-critique.
